metadata.source: git log analysis + session JSONL chunks
metadata.scope_note: 303 includes non-Liminal repo commits from the same git history; 294 are Liminal-specific

kai_agent.description: OpenClaw agent (Era 1) - autonomous coding agent running a task-job pipeline
behavioral_patterns: Relentlessly sequential: implement component N, then test component N, move to N+1
Self-verification loops: commits 019/020 both verify test coverage percentages
Test-obsessed: spent 8 of 29 commits on testing infrastructure
Near-zero creative latitude: every commit follows the task-job template
Rapid fire: sub-6-minute average cycle time suggests pre-planned batch execution
notable_commits: feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-019): Verified >92% test coverage exceeds 80% requirement
feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-019): Achieved >95% coverage for RalphLoop and >92% overall coverage
feat(task-job-1772343681762-kai-020): Super-testing verification and final cleanup

cursor_agent.description: Cursor IDE AI assistant (Era 2) - bulk feature addition and scaffolding
behavioral_patterns: Scaffolding-focused: 8 of 15 commits are 'Add X' - new modules, not refinements
Bulk dumps: initial burst of 10 commits in under 3 minutes (00:29 to 00:35)
Quality pass: single large 'Fix 34-item codebase audit' commit suggests bulk generation then retroactive cleanup
Broad but shallow: touched generators, GUI, TUI, tests, config, docs - everything at once
Deletion-averse: only 1,290 lines removed vs 19,785 added (6.5% deletion ratio)
Stopped mid-day: last commit at 14:02, then silent - likely human switched to Claude Code
commit_messages: [A] Enhance core system with self-improvement loops
[A] Enhance TUI with timeline and utilities
[A] Enhance generators, gallery, and preview server
[A] Add generated gallery and output files
[A] Temporarily remove GitHub workflows for push
[A] Add 4 creative coding generators: noise/flow fields, GLSL shaders, Three.js, LLM music
[A] Fix 34-item codebase audit: security, logic bugs, architecture, performance

claude_code.description: Claude Code CLI agent (Eras 3-9) - sustained development with co-authorship attribution

co_authorship.note: 173 commits include Kai/Cursor era commits that were co-authored; Claude Code specific is 158

memory_system.purpose: Persistent cross-session context for Claude Code agent

kai.observation: Machine-like precision but shallow - implemented to spec, verified to metric, never questioned requirements

cursor.observation: Maximum surface area, minimum depth - scaffolded everything, tested almost nothing, left a 34-item audit bill

claude_code.observation: Self-correcting over time - 20% fix rate shows iterative quality, hooks enforce process, memory preserves lessons

non_co_authored_breakdown.note: Non-co-authored Claude Code commits cluster in two periods: early (before co-author convention established) and recent (April 1 burst)

kai.consistency: mechanically uniform - every message follows exact template

claude_code.consistency: conventional commits with scope, varied quality

F_fix_commits.message: [F] Fix isConfigured for local providers + type-only imports
[F] Fix flatted DoS vulnerability via pnpm override

I_import_commits.message: [I] Add all Atelier + Liminal creative outputs to inbox
[I] Phase 1: native improvements + Wave 1-5 evolution merge cleanup

narrative_insights: THREE AGENTS, THREE PHILOSOPHIES: Kai was an assembly line - 29 commits in 2.8 hours, mechanically executing a task list with sub-6-minute cycle times. Cursor was a landscaper - dumped 15 commits of scaffolding in one afternoon, adding modules faster than it could test them (the '34-item audit' commit is the tell). Claude Code is the architect - 259 commits over 31 days, with a self-improving infrastructure of 26 hooks, 6 memory files, and persistent cross-session context.

THE CO-AUTHORSHIP TELL: 178 of 303 commits carry 'Co-Authored-By: Claude' - a transparency marker that reveals which sessions were AI-driven. But the 125 without co-authorship include the Kai era (29), Cursor era (15), and a suspicious April 1 burst of 50 commits. The non-co-authored commits cluster when human operators were more hands-on or when the co-author convention wasn't yet established.

VELOCITY IS NOT QUALITY: Kai had the highest commit velocity (10.5/hour) but the narrowest scope - it implemented to spec and stopped. Cursor had moderate velocity (1.1/hour) but left technical debt visible in the '34-item audit' commit. Claude Code is the slowest (0.34/hour) but the only agent that built its own quality enforcement infrastructure.

THE HOOKS ARE THE DIFFERENCE: 26 custom hooks represent Claude Code's meta-cognitive layer - something neither Kai nor Cursor had. They enforce process (wiring-checklist, process-enforcement), quality (code-quality, review-checklist), and safety (destructive-guard, file-protection). This is an agent that learned to police itself.

SESSION DEEPENING: Claude Code's sessions got longer over time - Era 3 averaged 12 messages per session, Era 8 averaged 31. The agent was being trusted with increasingly complex multi-step tasks as the human-LLM collaboration matured. The quiet era (Era 6, only 2 sessions) is the breath between waves.

THE APRIL 1 ANOMALY: 53 commits on a single day (50 non-co-authored) suggests either a burst of human-driven work or a session where the co-author convention was skipped. This is the most commit-dense day in the entire project history.

LINE ECONOMICS: Across all agents, 484,818 lines were added and 190,988 removed (2.54:1 ratio). Cursor was the most insertion-heavy (15.3:1 ratio), suggesting greenfield scaffolding. Claude Code had the most balanced ratio (2.56:1), indicating both building and refactoring.

MEMORY AS CONTINUITY: The 6 memory files in Claude Code's project directory are a form of externalized cognition - indexing rules, LIR status, known flaky tests, pipeline notes. This is how an agent without persistent state maintains continuity across 71 sessions spanning 13 active days.
